seo_title: Current Time in Austin, TX — CST/CDT (UTC−6/−5)
seo_description: Current time in Austin, Texas. Central Time Zone — CST (UTC-6) in winter, CDT (UTC-5) during DST. Time differences and business hours for Austin, TX.

faq.question: What time is it in Austin, Texas right now?
faq.answer: Austin, Texas, USA uses Central Standard Time (CST, UTC-6) in winter and Central Daylight Time (CDT, UTC-5) during Daylight Saving Time. The IANA identifier is America/Chicago. Austin is on the same time as Dallas, Houston, Chicago, and New Orleans — 1 hour behind New York and 6 hours behind London during standard time. The live clock above shows the current Austin time.

faq.question: What time zone is Austin, Texas in?
faq.answer: Austin, Texas is in the Central Time Zone (CT). It uses Central Standard Time (CST, UTC-6) from the first Sunday in November to the second Sunday in March, and Central Daylight Time (CDT, UTC-5) the rest of the year. The IANA identifier is America/Chicago. Austin shares its time zone with all of Texas (except El Paso), Chicago, Dallas, and Houston.

faq.question: Does Austin observe daylight saving time?
faq.answer: Yes. Austin, Texas observes Daylight Saving Time. Clocks spring forward 1 hour on the second Sunday in March at 2:00 AM (CST → CDT, UTC-6 → UTC-5) and fall back on the first Sunday in November. Texas follows the standard US DST schedule.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Austin and New York?
faq.answer: Austin (CST/CDT) is always 1 hour behind New York (EST/EDT). Both cities observe DST on the same schedule, keeping the gap constant year-round. When it is noon in New York, it is 11:00 AM in Austin. The best overlap for business calls is 10:00 AM–6:00 PM Eastern (9:00 AM–5:00 PM Central).

faq.question: What is the time difference between Austin and Los Angeles?
faq.answer: Austin (CST/CDT) is always 2 hours ahead of Los Angeles (PST/PDT). Both cities observe DST on the same schedule, keeping the 2-hour gap constant year-round. When it is 9:00 AM in Los Angeles, it is 11:00 AM in Austin. Austin's tech sector (Silicon Hills) heavily coordinates with Silicon Valley on this 2-hour offset.

faq.question: What are typical business hours in Austin, Texas?
faq.answer: Standard business hours in Austin are 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM CT, Monday through Friday. Austin's booming tech scene (sometimes called 'Silicon Hills') often runs flexible hours from 8:00 AM to 7:00 PM CT. The South by Southwest (SXSW) festival in March is a major event impacting city schedules. The University of Texas at Austin campus area sees peak activity 8:00 AM–9:00 PM during semesters.

faq.question: Is Austin in the same time zone as Dallas and Houston?
faq.answer: Yes. Austin, Dallas, and Houston are all in the Central Time Zone (CST/CDT), IANA identifier America/Chicago. They all observe the same DST schedule, so there is no time difference between these Texas cities. All three are 1 hour behind New York and 2 hours ahead of Los Angeles.
